Validating onet2r outputs

Source: vignettes/output-validation.Rmd
output-validation.Rmd

The package test suite checks implementation details with mocked responses and small deterministic examples. For real projects, it is also useful to inspect output shape: Are expected columns present? Are rows non-empty where they should be? Are list endpoints returning the fields users need?

onet2r includes a practical validation script that maintainers can run from the source tree with Rscript inst/examples/validate-outputs.R. From an installed package, source the file returned by:

system.file("examples", "validate-outputs.R", package = "onet2r")
#> [1] "C:/Users/alexfarach/AppData/Local/R/win-library/4.5/onet2r/examples/validate-outputs.R"

What the Script Checks

The validation script always checks deterministic local outputs:

  • reading bundled OEWS sample data
  • joining O*NET occupation codes to OEWS estimates
  • computing employment- and wage-weighted O*NET summaries
  • configuring cache and rate-limit settings

When ONET_API_KEY is available, it also performs live smoke checks across the main exported O*NET functions. Each result is inspected for expected type, minimum row count, and important columns.

Example: Local Validation without an API Key 

library(onet2r)
library(tibble)

sample_oews <- system.file(
  "extdata",
  "oews-national-sample.csv",
  package = "onet2r"
)

oews <- onet_oews_national(2024, path = sample_oews)

validation <- tibble(
  check = c(
    "OEWS output is a tibble",
    "OEWS has wage and employment columns",
    "join adds normalized SOC code"
  ),
  passed = c(
    inherits(oews, "tbl_df"),
    all(c("occ_code", "tot_emp", "a_median") %in% names(oews)),
    TRUE
  )
)

occupations <- tibble(
  code = c("15-1252.00", "29-1141.00"),
  title = c("Software Developers", "Registered Nurses")
)

joined <- onet_join_oews(occupations, oews = oews)
validation$passed[3] <- all(c("soc_code", "tot_emp", "a_median") %in% names(joined))

validation
#> # A tibble: 3 × 2
#>   check                                passed
#>   <chr>                                <lgl> 
#> 1 OEWS output is a tibble              TRUE  
#> 2 OEWS has wage and employment columns TRUE  
#> 3 join adds normalized SOC code        TRUE
head(joined, 5)
#> # A tibble: 2 × 9
#>   code       title  soc_code  year oews_type occ_title tot_emp a_median h_median
#>   <chr>      <chr>  <chr>    <int> <chr>     <chr>       <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>
#> 1 15-1252.00 Softw… 15-1252   2024 national  Software… 1847900   133080     64.0
#> 2 29-1141.00 Regis… 29-1141   2024 national  Register… 3175400    93070     44.8

Example: Live Validation Status

Live validation only runs when a user supplies an API key. The site still executes this chunk and reports whether live checks are available in the current environment.

tibble(
  check = "ONET_API_KEY available for live API validation",
  passed = nzchar(Sys.getenv("ONET_API_KEY"))
)
#> # A tibble: 1 × 2
#>   check                                          passed
#>   <chr>                                          <lgl> 
#> 1 ONET_API_KEY available for live API validation TRUE

The goal is not to replace unit tests. It is to give maintainers and users a repeatable, practical way to confirm that live API outputs still match the shapes used in examples, vignettes, and downstream analyses.

On this page

Developed by Alex Farach.
